title: write_cxxrtl: missing parameter when instantiating a Verilog blackbox module.
body:
### Version

Yosys 0.36+8 (git sha1 e6021b2b4, clang 16.0.6 -fPIC -Os)

### On which OS did this happen?

Linux

### Reproduction Steps

Given this `repro.v` file:

```verilog
(* cxxrtl_blackbox, cxxrtl_template = "DATA_BITS" *)
module serial_tx(...);
        parameter DATA_BITS = 8;
        (* cxxrtl_width = "DATA_BITS" *) input [DATA_BITS - 1:0] data;
endmodule

(* top *)
module top();
        serial_tx #(.DATA_BITS(16)) tx (.data(16'b0));
endmodule
```

Run `yosys repro.v -o repro.cc`.


### Expected Behavior

A `repro.cc` file is created.

### Actual Behavior

Yosys fails with the following error:
```
ERROR: Cell `top.tx' does not have a parameter `DATA_BITS', which is required by the templated module `$paramod\serial_tx\DATA_BITS=s32'00000000000000000000000000010000'.
```

The error can also be reproduced by these commands:
```
read_verilog -defer repro.v
write_cxxrtl repro.cc
```

However, removing `-defer` will produce the expected behavior.
code_blocks: ERROR: Cell `top.tx' does not have a parameter `DATA_BITS', which is required by the templated module `$paramod\serial_tx\DATA_BITS=s32'00000000000000000000000000010000'.
